title: sonchus arvensis subsp. uliginosus
intro: Sonchus arvensis subsp. uliginosus, also known as marsh sow-thistle, is a subspecies of the sow-thistle plant, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the body. The health benefits of marsh sow-thistle include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic, making it a valuable remedy for treating various health issues. Marsh sow-thistle can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
